channel.title: Gradient Descent
channel.description: Daily AI intelligence briefing — data science, finance, sports, and research.

item.title: Gradient Descent — Sunday, April 26, 2026
item.description: The cost of using AI tools has dropped to the point where it's now a no-brainer for many applications, much like when cloud storage became so cheap that it killed the hard drive market. This shift is already being felt on trading desks, where firms a
item.content:encoded:
Gradient Descent · Sunday, April 26, 2026 · 10 articles · ~3 min read

Today's Brief

The cost of using AI tools has dropped to the point where it's now a no-brainer for many applications, much like when cloud storage became so cheap that it killed the hard drive market. This shift is already being felt on trading desks, where firms are rethinking their strategies now that they can afford to automate more tasks and process larger datasets. As you consider your own team's workflow, ask yourself: what's the one task that's been too expensive to automate until now, and how would changing that change your entire approach?

📊 AI & Data Tools

A Coding Tutorial on Datashader

Datashader can render massive datasets quickly, and a new tutorial shows how to use it in Google Colab. This changes the game for data visualization, making it possible to work with huge datasets in a way that was previously impossible, so you can revisit your old projects and see if Datashader can help you find new insights. → Read

A Coding Implementation on kvcached

Kvcached is a dynamic KV-cache implementation that can transform GPU memory usage for large language models, and a new tutorial shows how to use it. This changes the way you think about deploying language models, making it possible to use more complex models without breaking the bank, so you can start experimenting with kvcached to see what it can do for your projects. → Read

OpenAI unveils GPT-5.5

OpenAI has announced GPT-5.5, a new model that can work through complex tasks autonomously, but it comes at double the API price. This changes the decision of whether to upgrade to the new model, forcing you to weigh the benefits of the new capabilities against the increased cost, so you should calculate whether the new features are worth the extra expense for your specific use case. → Read

📈 AI in Finance

Will AI save the U.S. fiscal situation?

A small increase in productivity growth due to AI could raise the fundamental value of U.S. government debt by $1.3 trillion. This changes the way you think about the impact of AI on the economy, making it clear that even small changes can have huge effects, so you should start thinking about how AI could affect your own investments and financial plans. → Read

A Year Later, Trump’s ‘Most Exclusive’ Memecoin Event Is a Lot Less Exclusive

A memecoin event that was once exclusive is now less so, causing outrage and raising questions about the value of such events. This changes the way you think about the value of exclusive events, making it clear that they can lose their luster quickly, so you should be skeptical of any event that promises exclusive access to something that may not be as valuable as it seems. → Read

Some young Americans scale back dating as costs and apps add pressure

Dating is becoming a financial decision for young Americans, with higher costs and paid apps leading many to cut back on dates. This changes the way you think about the dating market, making it clear that financial considerations are playing a bigger role, so you should consider how this shift could affect your own social life or business plans. → Read

🏃 AI in Sports

De Zerbi surprised Spurs have the 'right spirit' to survive

Tottenham's coach is surprised that his team has the right spirit to survive, despite a worrying injury to a key player. This changes the way you think about team dynamics, making it clear that a team's spirit can be just as important as its talent, so you should consider how to build a strong team culture in your own organization. → Read

Late goals, pitch invasions and a wild ride from despair to joy

A remarkable football season ended in dramatic fashion, with a team winning the title in the last minute of the game. This changes the way you think about the excitement of sports, making it clear that even the most unexpected events can happen, so you should be prepared for anything when watching or participating in sports. → Read

FIFPro, the state of its finances, and how will it deal with a new rival global players' union?

The International Association of Footballers is facing a new rival union, which could change the way it operates and negotiates with FIFA. This changes the way you think about the power dynamics of sports unions, making it clear that new challenges can arise at any time, so you should stay informed about the latest developments in the sports world. → Read

🎧 Podcasts

BI 236 Liset de la Prida: Neurons, Ripples, and Manifolds

A new podcast episode explores the latest research on neurons, ripples, and manifolds, and how they relate to brain function. This changes the way you think about the complexities of the brain, making it clear that there is still much to be learned, so you should keep up with the latest research and discoveries in the field of neuroscience. → Listen

Gradient Descent is free and automated. Browse the full archive or subscribe to get it every morning.

item.title: Gradient Descent — Saturday, April 25, 2026
item.description: It's like the hard drive market all over again: AI running costs are dropping so fast that 'is it worth automating?' is no longer a real question, which is why you're seeing labs and companies rush to release new, giant models like DeepSeek-V4 that c

item.title: Gradient Descent — Friday, April 24, 2026
item.description: The commoditisation of high-scoring language models is forcing a reevaluation of the human-in-the-loop paradigm, which is why three separate releases this week targeted full-stack automation. This, in turn, has implications for the sports performance

item.title: Gradient Descent — Thursday, April 23, 2026
item.description: Inference cost curves are now driving product decisions faster than capability benchmarks, which is why every major lab is shipping automation tools this week. This convergence of AI and automation will have a second-order consequence on athletic per

item.title: Gradient Descent — Wednesday, April 22, 2026
item.description: The convergence of open-source AI tools and research is driving a structural shift in the industry, where labs are now prioritizing accessibility and transparency over proprietary models, which is why you're seeing a surge in releases like ml-intern

item.title: Gradient Descent — Tuesday, April 21, 2026
item.description: Inference cost curves are now driving product decisions faster than capability benchmarks, which is why every major lab shipped a small model this week. This shift is likely to impact athletic performance analysis, as more accessible AI tools enable

item.title: Gradient Descent — Monday, April 20, 2026
item.description: Inference cost curves are now driving product decisions faster than capability benchmarks, which is why every major lab is rethinking how large language models are served at scale. This shift has second-order consequences for capital markets, where t

item.title: Gradient Descent — Sunday, April 19, 2026
item.description: Reduced inference cost curves are driving the rapid development of smaller, more efficient AI models, which is why multiple labs have released compact models this week. This shift is likely to have a second-order consequence on capital markets, where

item.title: Gradient Descent — Saturday, April 18, 2026
item.description: Consider the potential risks of AI-driven technological transformation in finance and sports, as well as the limitations of current AI evaluation methods, and how these factors may impact your data analysis and decision-making processes.
